# Popover

A trigger and a free-form panel in the top layer — anchored under the button, light-dismissed, focus handled. Same machinery as the dropdown menu, pointed at arbitrary content.

```html
<button
  type="button"
  popovertarget="pop-dimensions"
  class="inline-flex h-9 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-input bg-transparent px-4 text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap text-foreground transition-colors hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [anchor-name:--pop-dimensions]"
>
  <svg class="size-3.5 text-muted-foreground" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true">
    <line x1="21" x2="14" y1="4" y2="4"></line>
    <line x1="10" x2="3" y1="4" y2="4"></line>
    <line x1="21" x2="12" y1="12" y2="12"></line>
    <line x1="8" x2="3" y1="12" y2="12"></line>
    <line x1="21" x2="16" y1="20" y2="20"></line>
    <line x1="12" x2="3" y1="20" y2="20"></line>
    <line x1="14" x2="14" y1="2" y2="6"></line>
    <line x1="8" x2="8" y1="10" y2="14"></line>
    <line x1="16" x2="16" y1="18" y2="22"></line>
  </svg>
  Dimensions
</button>

<div
  id="pop-dimensions"
  popover
  class="m-auto w-72 scale-95 rounded-lg border border-border bg-popover p-4 text-popover-foreground opacity-0 shadow-lg transition-[opacity,scale,overlay,display] transition-discrete duration-150 open:scale-100 open:opacity-100 starting:open:scale-95 starting:open:opacity-0 [position-anchor:--pop-dimensions] [position-area:block-end_span-inline-end] [position-try-fallbacks:flip-block] supports-[anchor-name:--a]:m-0 supports-[anchor-name:--a]:mt-1.5 supports-[anchor-name:--a]:origin-top-left"
>
  <div class="grid gap-4">
    <div class="space-y-1">
      <h3 class="text-sm leading-none font-medium">Dimensions</h3>
      <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">Set the size of the selected layer.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="grid grid-cols-[4.5rem_1fr] items-center gap-x-3 gap-y-2">
      <label for="pop-width" class="text-sm">Width</label>
      <input
        id="pop-width"
        type="text"
        inputmode="numeric"
        value="480"
        class="h-8 w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-2.5 text-sm transition-colors focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-1 focus-visible:outline-ring"
      />
      <label for="pop-max" class="text-sm">Max. width</label>
      <input
        id="pop-max"
        type="text"
        inputmode="numeric"
        value="960"
        class="h-8 w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-2.5 text-sm transition-colors focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-1 focus-visible:outline-ring"
      />
      <label for="pop-height" class="text-sm">Height</label>
      <input
        id="pop-height"
        type="text"
        inputmode="numeric"
        value="240"
        class="h-8 w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-2.5 text-sm transition-colors focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-1 focus-visible:outline-ring"
      />
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Popover vs Dropdown Menu

Both float a panel in the top layer, both anchor under a trigger, both dismiss on Esc and outside
click. The difference is the *contents*. A [Dropdown Menu](/components/dropdown-menu) is a list of
actions. A popover holds whatever you want: a little form, a set of controls, a preview. When the
panel has inputs and labels rather than a column of commands, reach for this.

## How it works

- The `popover` attribute + `popovertarget` on the button give you top-layer rendering, toggling,
  Esc and click-outside dismiss. No JavaScript, no focus-trap library. Tab moves into the fields;
  Esc returns to the trigger.
- CSS anchor positioning pins the panel under the button: `[anchor-name:--pop-dimensions]` on the
  trigger, then `[position-anchor:--pop-dimensions]` + `[position-area:block-end_span-inline-end]`
  on the panel ("below the anchor, growing to the right").
- `[position-try-fallbacks:flip-block]` flips the panel above when there's no room below.
- The inputs are plain `<input>`s. Because the panel lives in the top layer, they focus, type and
  tab exactly as they would in normal flow. The popover isn't a rendering trick that traps them.

## The fallback story

Without the Popover API the panel renders in normal document flow, always visible, and the button
does nothing, so keep the markup somewhere sensible in the source order. Without anchor positioning
the popover still opens, just centered in the viewport (the top-layer default) instead of pinned to
the trigger; that's why the base classes keep `m-auto` and only the offset is wrapped in a
`supports-[anchor-name:--a]:` guard.

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

- **Popover API** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Popover_API))
  Without it: Content renders in normal document flow, always visible, and the trigger does nothing.
- **CSS anchor positioning** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_anchor_positioning))
  Without it: The popover opens centered in the viewport (the top-layer default) instead of attached to its trigger.
- **Entry/exit animations (allow-discrete + @starting-style)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/transition-behavior))
  Without it: Elements appear and disappear instantly. Nothing breaks — you just lose the animation.
